title: NaN should be avoided in comparisons
description: "NaN" should not be used in comparisonsAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEWhy is this an issue?More InfoIn JavaScript,NaNstands for "Not-a-Number." It is a special value representing a numeric data type that is not a valid number.NaNis returned as a result when an arithmetic operation or mathematical function is performed, and the result is undefined or unrepresentable as a valid number.Comparing a value withNaNin JavaScript can be problematic because of the wayNaNbehaves in comparison operations. The reason is thatNaNis not equal to any value, including itself, and this behavior can lead to unexpected results.const a = NaN; if (a === NaN) { // Noncompliant: Always false console.log("a is not a number"); // This is
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.message: NaN should not be used in comparisons. Use isNaN() or Number.isNaN() instead.
